provisions.evidence: "Sorry, the owner hasn't given you permission to download this file."

Remove Trello link from rp-pl016121-codification.
Re-acquire the authentic source before any substantive codification.

laws.title: Public Law 17-124 | Law Enforcement Accountability Act (LEAA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. A free-standing, generally-applicable regulatory statute (use-of-force, canine, and de-escalation rules for law enforcement). No self-repeal or sunset; SEC2 anti-supersession clause. It amends no numbered USAR Code section by express strike/insert.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Law Enforcement Accountability Act' / 'LEAA'.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be referred to as the 'Law Enforcement Accountability Act' or 'LEAA'."

provisions.text_summary: Severability plus clause that Act may not be superseded unless later law expressly so declares.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent to supersede..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective upon passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be effective upon passage and signature of the President."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (apprehension, bite, canine handler, use of force, deadly force, resistance tiers, etc.).
provisions.evidence: "SEC4.DEFINITIONS For the purpose of this bill-"
provisions.notes: General permanent law; candidate for a new section in a law-enforcement title (no existing target given).

provisions.text_summary: Rules for tactical/non-tactical canine deployment, call-off, handler duties, medical treatment.
provisions.evidence: "Canine deployments can be authorised for the following assignments..."
provisions.notes: Two SEC5 headings appear (drafting duplication: Canines and Use of Force).

provisions.text_summary: Objective-reasonableness use-of-force framework, de-escalation, deadly-force limits, medical aid, duty to intervene/report.
provisions.evidence: "law enforcement shall only use force that is objectively reasonable."
provisions.notes: Duplicate SEC5 numbering in source.

provisions.text_summary: Attorney General and agency leadership to enact enforcement policies.
provisions.evidence: "The Attorney General of the United States, along with other Department and Agency Leadership shall enact policies and procedures to enforce the provisions of this act."
provisions.notes: No SEC6 in source (gap).

current_implementation.detail: Draft added only a boilerplate note at t18/s1 and declared 'Current'. Because this is substantive permanent general law, it should be enacted as real Code section(s), not merely a note. No quoted-content dump present; Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Codify as a new permanent chapter/sections (use-of-force + canine standards) in the appropriate law-enforcement placement rather than a bare note.
Remove Trello link from rp-pl017124-codification.
Correct duplicated SEC5 numbering and the missing SEC6 in editorial notes.

laws.title: Public Law 17-125 | We Don't Read Minds Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing directive requiring each executive department's Inspector General to submit monthly status reports to Congress; penalty for noncompliance. No self-repeal or sunset. Listed under 'Repealed Laws' but body shows no repeal of itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'We Don't Read Minds Act'.
provisions.evidence: "may also be referred to as the 'We Don't Read Minds Act'"

provisions.text_summary: Purpose: mandate monthly Cabinet updates to Congress.
provisions.evidence: "to mandate that the Cabinet of the United States deliver monthly updates..."

provisions.text_summary: Each executive department's Inspector General must submit monthly reports to Congress.
provisions.evidence: "the Inspector General from each executive department must be tasked with delivering and submitting monthly reports to the Congress..."
provisions.notes: Reporting duty; candidate for a permanent section (likely Title 5).

provisions.text_summary: House may open inquiry/investigation and fine a non-reporting OIG up to $10,000, plus further punishment.
provisions.evidence: "the OIG being fined of up to $10,000 in USD and any further punishment deemed necessary by the House."

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately after passage and Presidential signature.
provisions.evidence: "This bill will take effect immediately after passage through both houses of Congress and a signature from the President."
provisions.notes: Section numbered 6 though prior is 3 (drafting gap).

current_implementation.detail: Draft added a note at t5/s101 and declared 'Repealed or superseded' with no supporting evidence in the source (source shows no self-repeal). Substantive reporting mandate warrants an actual Code section. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Codify the IG monthly-reporting mandate as a permanent Title 5 section rather than a note; verify against later batch laws whether any repeal exists before marking repealed.
Remove 'Repealed or superseded' assertion unless a repealing later law is identified.
Remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-126 | Special Events Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Establishes special-event permitting authority in the Department of Commerce and Labor for the District of Columbia. Local/District-focused regulatory scheme; no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Special Events Act' / 'SEA'.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Special Events Act' or 'SEA'."

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective upon passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: "effective upon passage and signature of the President."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of permit, special event, forgery.
provisions.evidence: "'Special Event' means any sports events, pageants, public spectator attractions..."
provisions.notes: Numbered SEC5 (no SEC4); three separate SEC5 blocks in source (Definitions, Establishing Permits, Enforcement).

provisions.text_summary: DOCAL power to issue DC special-event permits (max 2 hours), permit contents, database, transmission to police, denial grounds, application requirements.
provisions.evidence: "issue permits for special events within the district of columbia, for a time not exceeding 2 hours."
provisions.notes: Purely District-of-Columbia administrative permitting; ordinarily not permanent U.S. Code.

provisions.text_summary: Forgery of a permit is a misdemeanour (fine up to $15,000 [text says 'fifty thousand'] and 4-day imprisonment); revocation/blacklist; enforcement authority.
provisions.evidence: "Any forgery of a Special Events Permit shall be classed as a misdemeanour..."
provisions.notes: Internal inconsistency: '$15,000' written after 'fifty thousand dollars'.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t50/s1 (Title 50 War & National Defense) which is a poor subject-matter anchor for a DC special-events permitting scheme; declared 'Repealed or superseded' without source support. Trello link present. Note: PL-019-144 (PAMA) later references PL-17-126 as still operative, undercutting a repeal claim.

recommended_actions: Exclude from permanent Code as local/District regulatory law; keep a concise honest historical note (not at Title 50).
Do not assert repeal absent a repealing instrument.
Remove Trello link; fix mis-anchored Title 50 placement.

laws.title: Public Law 17-127 | Border Security Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Sweeping amendatory immigration statute: SEC5 repeals all of Title 8 in its entirety, then SEC6-SEC13 rewrite numerous 8 U.S. Code sections with express 'shall now read as' replacement text. Numerous determinable textual amendments. No self-repeal/sunset. (Listed 'Repealed Laws' but source contains no self-repeal; PL-19-147 later repeals 8 USC 1102, a downstream change to be sequenced.)

provisions.text_summary: General provision incorporating PL 1-1 conventions.
provisions.evidence: "Pursuant to Public Law 1-1, codes listed in this act shall be applicable section(s)"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals all of Title 8 of the U.S. Code in its entirety.
provisions.exact_change: Repeal entire Title 8 (then re-enact enumerated sections via SEC6-13).
provisions.evidence: "U.S.Code: Title 8 is repealed in its entirety."
provisions.notes: Blanket title-wide repeal, immediately followed by re-enactment of specific sections.

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1101 rewritten: definitions (roblox-group 'United States', alien, admission, national security, alien terrorist, immigrant, etc.).
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 1101 text with the SEC6 definitional list ('8 U.S.Code 1101 shall now read as ...').
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1101 shall now read as; (1) As used in this chapter-"
provisions.notes: Determinable full-replacement text supplied.

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1551 rewritten to establish the Citizenship and Immigration Service under DHS.
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 1551 with SEC7 text creating the CIS, Director duties, reporting.
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1551 shall now read as; 8U.S.Code1551- Citizenship and Immigration Service"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1102 rewritten (diplomatic/semidiplomatic immunities; Vienna Convention via 22 USC 254b).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1102 shall now read as;"
provisions.notes: NB: PL-19-147 SEC17 later repeals section 1102 in its entirety - sequence accordingly.

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1103 rewritten (Powers and Duties of the Secretary of Homeland Security).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1103 shall now read as; 8U.S.Code1103- Powers and Duties of the Secretary"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1105 rewritten (internal security; NCIC data exchange).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1105 shall now read as;"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1182 rewritten (qualifications/inadmissibility; alternative-account and FTO-association grounds).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1182 shall read as;"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1189 rewritten (designation of foreign terrorist organisations).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1189 shall now read as;"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1532 rewritten (removal court; designated judge; attorney panel).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1532 shall now read as;"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1533 rewritten (application for removal).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1533 shall now read as;"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1534 rewritten (removal hearing procedure).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1534 shall now read as;"

provisions.text_summary: 8 U.S. Code 1481 rewritten (loss of nationality; cross-refs 18 USC 2383/2384).
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code1481 shall now read as;"
provisions.notes: References 18 USC 2383/2384 (crimes) as cross-citations, not amendments.

current_implementation.detail: Draft added a single note (placed oddly at t8/s1101/i/2) and made NO actual textual amendments to sections 1101,1102,1103,1105,1182,1189,1481,1532,1533,1534,1551 despite express 'shall now read as' replacements. This is exactly a case where notes cannot substitute for required textual amendments. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Perform actual textual amendments (amend-existing-text) to each of 8 USC 1101, 1102, 1103, 1105, 1182, 1189, 1481, 1532, 1533, 1534, 1551 with the SEC6-13 replacement text.
Record the SEC5 blanket Title-8 repeal as repeal-marking, reconciled with the section re-enactments (net effect: only enumerated sections survive/are rewritten).
Sequence PL-19-147's later repeal of section 1102 after this Act.
Remove Trello link; fix note placement.

laws.title: Public Law 17-128 | Impeachment Procedures and Protocols Act (IPPA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Congressional-procedure statute establishing impeachment inquiry/trial rules; SEC9 makes a determinable textual amendment to 28 U.S. Code 1365 (Congressional actions). No self-repeal; SEC2 anti-supersession. ('sunset' keyword in manifest is a false positive; no sunset clause exists.)

provisions.evidence: "'Impeachment Procedures and Protocols Act' or 'IPPA'"

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective on passage/signature.
provisions.evidence: "effective upon passage and signature of the President."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (public office/official, impeachment, conviction, etc.).
provisions.evidence: "SEC4.DEFINITIONS For the purpose of this bill-"

provisions.text_summary: Impeachment inquiry initiation, subpoena authority, House vote, impeachment managers, Senate conviction procedures.
provisions.evidence: "the committee...shall have the authority to issue subpoenas..."
provisions.notes: Internal congressional rules; may be statutory-note or Title 2 sections.

provisions.text_summary: 28 U.S. Code 1365 rewritten (enforcement of congressional subpoenas; DDC jurisdiction; penalties; waiver rules).
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 1365 with SEC9 text ('28 U.S.Code 1365 shall now read as- 28 U.S.Code 1365- Congressional actions ...'); references 28 USC 1657 as cross-cite.
provisions.evidence: "28U.S.Code1365 shall now read as-"
provisions.notes: Determinable full-replacement text supplied.

provisions.text_summary: Compliance duties for subpoena recipients; rule of construction preserving inherent authority.
provisions.evidence: "Nothing in this Act may be interpreted to limit or constrain Congress' inherent authority..."

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t2/s1 and declared 'Repealed or superseded' without source support; failed to make the required textual amendment to 28 USC 1365. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Amend 28 USC 1365 (amend-existing-text) with the SEC9 replacement text.
Codify SEC4-8, 10-11 as Title 2 procedural sections or a consolidated statutory note.

laws.title: Public Law 17-129 | Department of Education Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present (DOCX-extracted). Organic act establishing the Department of Education as an executive department; findings, purposes, definitions, officers, transfers, administrative provisions. Free-standing permanent general law; no self-repeal/sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Department of Education Act'.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the 'Department of Education Act'."

provisions.text_summary: Congressional findings on education.
provisions.evidence: "The Congress finds that- education is fundamental..."

provisions.text_summary: Purposes of establishing the Department.
provisions.evidence: "the establishment of a Department of Education is in the public interest..."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (Department, Secretary, Deputy Secretary, function, office).
provisions.evidence: "As used in this Act...the term 'Department' means the Department of Education..."
provisions.notes: Organic-act text - Title 20.

provisions.text_summary: Establishment of the executive department and principal officers (Secretary, Deputy, Assistant Secretaries, General Counsel).
provisions.evidence: "There is established an executive department to be known as the Department of Education."

provisions.text_summary: Office of Career and Technical Education.
provisions.evidence: "There shall be in the Department an Office of Career, Technical, and Adult Education..."

provisions.text_summary: Transfers of agencies/functions from Dept. of Commerce & Labor and Dept. of Justice.
provisions.evidence: "There shall be transferred to the Secretary...all functions of the Secretary of Commerce and Labor...relating to such education."

provisions.text_summary: General administrative provisions (authority, delegation, reorganization, rules, seal, monthly report).
provisions.evidence: "In carrying out any function transferred by this Act, the Secretary...may exercise any authority available by law..."

provisions.text_summary: Construction; severability.
provisions.evidence: "shall be deemed severable from this Act and shall not affect the remainder thereof..."

provisions.text_summary: Reference: transferred-function references deemed to refer to the Secretary.
provisions.evidence: "reference in any other Federal law to any department...shall be deemed to refer to the Secretary..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective date; interim appointments (20-day acting).
provisions.evidence: "This bill will take effect immediately after passage..."

current_implementation.detail: Draft added an improper full-text quoted-content dump (note_char_length 15523; has_quoted_content_dump=true, sha reference present) and a Trello link, and declared 'Repealed or superseded' without support. This organic act should be enacted as real Title 20 sections, not dumped into a note.

recommended_actions: Codify as new permanent Title 20 sections (department establishment/officers/transfers); do not store the whole act as a quoted-content note dump.
Remove the full-text <quotedContent> dump and Trello link from rp-pl017129-codification.
Drop unsupported 'repealed' status; keep transfer/effective-date/savings statutory notes.

laws.title: Public Law 17-130 | Oath and Offices Protection (OOP)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing statute requiring officials to take the 5 U.S. Code 3331 oath within set timeframes, with forfeiture for failure; references 3331 as an existing anchor but does not restate/replace its text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'OOP'.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Oath and Offices Protection' or 'OOP'"

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective on passage/signature.
provisions.evidence: "effective upon passage and signature of the President."

provisions.text_summary: General provision (PL 1-1) and definitions; ties 'oath of office' to 5 USC 3331.
provisions.evidence: "The term 'oath of office' refers to the oath established in 5U.S.Code3331."
provisions.notes: Cross-reference to 3331, not an amendment of it.

provisions.text_summary: Oath must be taken within 5 days (elected), 48 hours (appointed), 24 hours (acting); failure forfeits office; excludes President/VP.
provisions.evidence: "Failure to be sworn in within the allotted timeframe shall result in said person forfeiting their position or office."
provisions.notes: Candidate for a new Title 5 section adjacent to 3331; NOT a textual amendment of 3331.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t50/s1 (Title 50) - wrong subject-matter anchor for a 5 USC 3331 oath statute - and declared 'Repealed'. Substantive rule warrants a Title 5 section. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Codify SEC6 oath-timing rule as a new Title 5 section near 3331 (not Title 50); add a cross-reference note at 3331.
Drop unsupported 'repealed' status.
Remove Trello link; correct mis-anchored placement.

laws.title: Public Law 17-131 | More Jobs Amendment
laws.status_basis: Full source present (DOCX). An amendment to a prior USAR public law (PL 15-100, 'Employment Rights and Limitations Act'), not to a numbered U.S. Code section. Amends specified subsections of PL 15-100 with determinable replacement text. No self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'More Jobs Amendment'.
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the 'More Jobs Amendment'"

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 15-100 Section 3, Subsection B(I) to read: two 'primary' + three 'secondary' positions; or four 'secondary' positions.
provisions.exact_change: Set section 3(B)(I) to read 'two "primary" positions and three "secondary" positions; or four "secondary" positions.'
provisions.evidence: "Section 3 of the Employment Rights and Limitations Act, Subsection B I, Public Law 15-100 shall be henceforth read;"
provisions.notes: Target is a prior public law; identify wherever PL 15-100 is codified (likely Title 29). If PL 15-100 is only a statutory note, amend that note text.

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 15-100 Section 5, Subsection F: no person may hold three+ concurrent agencies/positions unless constitutionally/statutorily authorized; plus limitation on holding a PAS office and an elected office simultaneously (except US Attorney/Assistant US Attorney).
provisions.exact_change: Replace section 5(F) and its limitation clause with the SEC2(b) text.
provisions.evidence: "Section 5, Subsection F, Public Law 15-100 shall be henceforth read;"

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately after passage/signature.
provisions.evidence: "This bill will take effect immediately after passage..."

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a note at t29/s201 with an improper full-text quoted-content dump (has_quoted_content_dump=true, sha ref) and Trello link, and declared 'Repealed'. The Act makes real amendments to PL 15-100's provisions, which must be effected in whatever text carries PL 15-100.

recommended_actions: Locate the codified/noted text of PL 15-100 and apply the SEC2 amendments as actual textual changes (amend-existing-text).
Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Drop unsupported 'repealed' status.
